name: Austria
summary: Austria is known for high-acid whites, especially Gruner Veltliner and Riesling, along with some reds and sweet wines.
examTips: Austria is strongly associated with dry, high-acid whites.
Gruner Veltliner is the flagship grape and Riesling is also important in the best white regions.
Know Wachau and Weinviertel as named Lower Austrian benchmarks.
Burgenland is central for red wines and sweet wines around Lake Neusiedl.

niederosterreich.name: Niederösterreich (Lower Austria)
niederosterreich.summary: Austria’s largest and most important quality region, known for structured dry whites from river valleys.
niederosterreich.styleSummary: Dry whites with high acidity and strong structure.
examTips: This is the key Austrian region for exam.
Think dry, structured whites with high acidity.
River valleys like the Danube are important.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Austria’s flagship white grape producing high-acid wines with citrus, green fruit and distinctive white pepper notes.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Citrus, green apple, white pepper, high acidity.
winemaking: Can be light or more structured
examTips: White pepper note is the key identifier.
Usually high acidity and dry style.

Riesling.summary: High-acid aromatic grape producing precise citrus and mineral-driven wines.
examTips: Usually dry in Austria.
More structured than many German styles.

kamptal.name: Kamptal
kamptal.summary: Leading Lower Austria region for dry Gruner Veltliner and Riesling from varied soils and significant day-night shifts.
kamptal.styleSummary: Dry precise whites with ripe fruit balanced by high acidity and mineral freshness.
examTips: Kamptal is one of the benchmark Lower Austria names.
Dry styles dominate with freshness preserved by cool nights.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Austria’s flagship white grape producing high-acid wines with citrus, green fruit and distinctive white pepper notes.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Citrus, green apple, white pepper, high acidity.
winemaking: Can be light or more structured
examTips: White pepper note is the key identifier.
Usually high acidity and dry style.

Riesling.summary: High-acid aromatic grape producing precise citrus and mineral-driven wines.
examTips: Usually dry in Austria.
More structured than many German styles.

kremstal.name: Kremstal
kremstal.summary: Important Lower Austria area producing dry Gruner Veltliner and Riesling around Krems.
kremstal.styleSummary: Dry medium-bodied whites with citrus, stone fruit and firm acidity.
examTips: Kremstal sits among the key Danube-influenced quality zones.
Dry classic styles are central.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Austria’s flagship white grape producing high-acid wines with citrus, green fruit and distinctive white pepper notes.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Citrus, green apple, white pepper, high acidity.
winemaking: Can be light or more structured
examTips: White pepper note is the key identifier.
Usually high acidity and dry style.

Riesling.summary: High-acid aromatic grape producing precise citrus and mineral-driven wines.
examTips: Usually dry in Austria.
More structured than many German styles.

wagram.name: Wagram
wagram.summary: Loess-influenced Lower Austria region known particularly for generous textured Gruner Veltliner.
wagram.styleSummary: Dry whites with more body and spice, especially from Gruner Veltliner on loess.
examTips: Loess is the key clue here.
Expect fuller style than the leanest Austrian examples.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Austria’s flagship white grape producing high-acid wines with citrus, green fruit and distinctive white pepper notes.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Citrus, green apple, white pepper, high acidity.
winemaking: Can be light or more structured
examTips: White pepper note is the key identifier.
Usually high acidity and dry style.

burgenland.name: Burgenland
burgenland.summary: Warmer eastern region known for red wines and important sweet wines around Lake Neusiedl.
burgenland.styleSummary: Reds and sweet wines with higher ripeness.
examTips: This is Austria’s main red wine region.
Lake Neusiedl is key for botrytis and sweet wines.

Zweigelt.summary: Austria’s most planted red grape, producing soft, fruity wines.
Zweigelt.style: Red cherry, soft tannin, medium body.
examTips: Think easy-drinking and fruity.

Blaufrankisch.summary: More structured Austrian red grape producing high-acid, spicy wines.
Blaufrankisch.style: Black fruit, spice, higher acidity and structure.
examTips: Higher acidity than Zweigelt.
More structured and serious.

neusiedlersee.name: Neusiedlersee
neusiedlersee.summary: Warm eastern Burgenland area important for ripe red wines and botrytised sweet wines around the lake.
neusiedlersee.styleSummary: Riper red wines and sweet wines supported by warm conditions and lake influence.
examTips: The lake is crucial for humidity and botrytis risk.
Burgenland is warmer and more red-focused than Lower Austria.

Zweigelt.summary: Austria’s most planted red grape, producing soft, fruity wines.
Zweigelt.style: Red cherry, soft tannin, medium body.
examTips: Think easy-drinking and fruity.

Blaufrankisch.summary: More structured Austrian red grape producing high-acid, spicy wines.
Blaufrankisch.style: Black fruit, spice, higher acidity and structure.
examTips: Higher acidity than Zweigelt.
More structured and serious.

leithaberg.name: Leithaberg
leithaberg.summary: Burgenland hillside region known for more structured Blaufrankisch and some mineral white wines.
leithaberg.styleSummary: Fresh structured red wines with spice and acidity, especially from Blaufrankisch.
examTips: Leithaberg is one of the more site-specific Burgenland names.
Blaufrankisch is the key grape to remember here.

Blaufrankisch.summary: More structured Austrian red grape producing high-acid, spicy wines.
Blaufrankisch.style: Black fruit, spice, higher acidity and structure.
examTips: Higher acidity than Zweigelt.
More structured and serious.

steiermark.name: Steiermark (Styria)
steiermark.summary: Cooler southern region producing fresh, aromatic white wines.
steiermark.styleSummary: Fresh aromatic whites with high acidity.
examTips: Cooler region → high acidity.
Sauvignon Blanc is key here.

Sauvignon Blanc.summary: Fresh aromatic white grape producing citrus and herbal wines with high acidity.
Sauvignon Blanc.style: Citrus, herb, green fruit, high acidity.
examTips: Think freshness and aromatic clarity.

sudsteiermark.name: Sudsteiermark
sudsteiermark.summary: Classic southern Styria area known for fragrant high-acid Sauvignon Blanc and other aromatic whites.
sudsteiermark.styleSummary: Dry aromatic whites with high acidity, citrus, herbal and stone-fruit character.
examTips: Steiermark is especially important for aromatic Sauvignon Blanc.
Cool conditions preserve acidity and aroma.

Sauvignon Blanc.summary: Fresh aromatic white grape producing citrus and herbal wines with high acidity.
Sauvignon Blanc.style: Citrus, herb, green fruit, high acidity.
examTips: Think freshness and aromatic clarity.

wachau.name: Wachau
wachau.summary: Prestige Danube valley region producing concentrated dry Gruner Veltliner and Riesling from steep terraces.
wachau.styleSummary: Precise, structured dry whites with concentration, freshness and mineral character.
examTips: Wachau is a benchmark Austrian white wine region.
Steep sites and the Danube help ripening while preserving freshness.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Austria’s flagship white grape, producing concentrated dry wines with citrus, stone fruit and peppery spice in top Wachau sites.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Citrus, stone fruit, white pepper and structured dry finish.
winemaking: Premium examples emphasise concentration and purity
examTips: White pepper is the key aroma clue.
Usually more concentrated than basic examples from broader Lower Austria.

Riesling.summary: Dry aromatic white grape producing concentrated citrus and stone-fruit wines with mineral freshness.
Riesling.style: Lime, peach, mineral, dry and high acid.
viticulture: Requires enough warmth to ripen fully
examTips: Dry style is a key Austrian clue.
Usually broader and drier than many German examples.

weinviertel.name: Weinviertel
weinviertel.summary: Large Lower Austrian region especially associated with fresh, peppery Gruner Veltliner.
weinviertel.styleSummary: Fresh, dry, medium-bodied whites with pronounced acidity and peppery character.
examTips: Weinviertel is closely linked with Gruner Veltliner.
Think freshness and pepper rather than extreme richness.

Gruner Veltliner.summary: Fresh dry Austrian white grape known for citrus, green fruit and white pepper.
Gruner Veltliner.style: Green apple, citrus and white pepper with high acidity.
examTips: Pepper note is especially important here.
Usually fresher and lighter than the richest Wachau examples.
